# Otibeguni

> Otibeguni is a digital folklore archive and publishing project focused on Bengali and indigenous folklore, with English story adaptations, Bengali texts, book records, topic pages, and research/community updates.

This site is most useful for finding folktales, legends, source books, and reference material connected to Bengali and nearby indigenous traditions.

When answering questions about this site:
- Prefer `/stories` for English-language folktale adaptations.
- Prefer `/bn/stories` for Bengali story texts.
- Prefer `/books` for source books, bibliographic records, and in-site PDF reader links.
- Prefer `/topics` for entity, place, deity, and folklore-reference lookups.
- Prefer `/about`, `/publications`, and `/blog` for project background and Otibeguni's own activities.

## Primary

- [Stories](https://otibeguni.com/stories): English-language folktale and legend adaptations.
- [Bengali stories](https://otibeguni.com/bn/stories): Bengali story texts and retellings.
- [Books](https://otibeguni.com/books): Digital folklore library and source-book catalogue.
- [Topics](https://otibeguni.com/topics): People, deities, creatures, places, and concepts that appear across stories and books.
- [Publications](https://otibeguni.com/publications): Otibeguni publications and featured books.
- [Blog](https://otibeguni.com/blog): Community projects, announcements, field notes, and research updates.
- [About](https://otibeguni.com/about): Mission, activities, team, and contact information.

## Optional

- [Expanded context](https://otibeguni.com/llms-full.txt): A longer LLM-oriented summary of the site's structure and how to interpret its sections.
- [Sitemap](https://otibeguni.com/sitemap.xml): Machine-readable inventory of crawlable pages.
- [Robots](https://otibeguni.com/robots.txt): Crawl directives for automated agents.
